This is a reproducible research project for validation of the nascent Spiritual Harm and Abuse Scale (SHAS), developed by Dan Koch. It aims to function as an evolving technical report to conceptualize and document various validity studies -- and, by extension, validity evidence -- for this scale. We hope some of these validity considerations and empirical studies amount to meaningful publishable contributions to the nascent research literature in spiritual abuse and/or the literature in religion, counseling psychology, and/or psychological measurement. The project is open source, for collaboration. It is built in R using the R Studio IDE (@R-base). No Excel, no SPSS, no Word. It is built in `bookdown` (@R-bookdown) and hosted at Github as a public repository at https://github.com/jackbhuber/SHAS. There it is served over Github Pages as a website -- https://jackhuber.org/SHAS/ -- for collaboration between Jack and Dan.
